---
title: "Sleep and memory workshop report"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    df-print: paged
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
library(yaml)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

res <- yaml::read_yaml("outputs/results/anova.yml")

desc <- bind_rows(lapply(res$descriptives, as.data.frame))
desc$sleep <- as.factor(desc$sleep)
desc$cue <- as.factor(desc$cue)

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return(NA_character_)
  if (p < .001) return("< .001")
  sprintf("%.3f", p)
}

fmt_f <- function(f) sprintf("%.2f", f)
fmt_pes <- function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x)
```

## Research question

Do sleep and cueing influence recall performance, and do they interact.

## Descriptives

```{r}
desc <- desc %>%
  mutate(
    mean = as.numeric(mean),
    sd = as.numeric(sd),
    mean = round(mean, 2),
    sd = round(sd, 2)
  ) %>%
  arrange(sleep, cue)

knitr::kable(desc, digits = 2)
```

## Inferential results

```{r}
a_sleep <- res$anova$sleep
a_cue <- res$anova$cue
a_int <- res$anova$interaction
```

Sleep main effect: *F*(`r a_sleep$df1`, `r a_sleep$df2`) = `r fmt_f(a_sleep$f)`, *p* = `r fmt_p(a_sleep$p)`, partial η² = `r fmt_pes(a_sleep$partial_eta_sq)`.

Cue main effect: *F*(`r a_cue$df1`, `r a_cue$df2`) = `r fmt_f(a_cue$f)`, *p* = `r fmt_p(a_cue$p)`, partial η² = `r fmt_pes(a_cue$partial_eta_sq)`.

Sleep × cue interaction: *F*(`r a_int$df1`, `r a_int$df2`) = `r fmt_f(a_int$f)`, *p* = `r fmt_p(a_int$p)`, partial η² = `r fmt_pes(a_int$partial_eta_sq)`.

## Plot

![](../outputs/figures/interaction.png)

## Notes on interpretation

Write a short paragraph interpreting the pattern in plain language. Use APA style where appropriate.

If you are unsure about assumptions or whether a follow-up test is required, say so clearly.
